items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation and preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения». It also correctly avoids turning «у Шпака / у посла» into simple possession and instead keeps the elliptical, accusatory linkage that drives the joke (each has “a corresponding item” in the suspicion chain). No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. It reproduces the era-clash humor: banal Soviet politeness formulas and repetitive self-introduction (“the Tsar”) instead of archaic royal speech, matching Bunsha’s drunken, bureaucratic voice. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the imperious, suddenly power-hungry tone that is funny precisely because it comes from a petty Soviet functionary playing autocrat. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Preserves the elevated/archaic melancholy and the comic insult (“старушка”) in a modern domestic setting. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Correctly keeps “demons” as the agent and the repeated phrasing that conveys panic and superstition, preserving the technology-as-sorcery joke. No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Maintains the mock-biblical proclamation and the religious framing that is key to the joke in an officially atheist Soviet context. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Captures the performative MC-style command that fits the scene’s anachronistic party energy; it’s a good functional equivalent rather than a flat literal “Everyone dances!”. No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the breezy, self-satisfied criminal cheerfulness and the idiomatic punch of the original. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant item “caviar” in English, preventing confusion while preserving the Soviet-satire inversion (cheap spread framed as exotic delicacy). No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Preserves the comic rhythm of the inventory, the status-item boasting, and the trailing self-correction feel (“...Three.”) that signals exaggeration/instability. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism that turns a frightening reality (psychiatric commitment) into shared absurdist comedy. No issues.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
